title: Platform Bed with Side Storage and 1x12 Divider Shelf
summary: A full-size platform bed built on a torsion-box-style 2x4 frame, with a divided storage tower attached down one side in place of a nightstand.
description:
The frame does double duty as both the bed's structure and the storage tower's mounting surface — the storage piece attaches to a dedicated support 2x4 added across the top of the inner frame, rather than hanging off the outer trim, so the weight of anything stored there loads into the bed's structural frame and not into a piece of finish trim.

The source is explicit about handling this as two separate assemblies: build and finish the platform and the storage tower separately, then bring both into the bedroom and join them there. A bed this size, fully assembled, won't fit through most doorways or up a stairwell — a lesson worth taking at face value rather than discovering partway up the stairs.

materials.name: Plywood, 1/2", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Platform base under the mattress; scrap material also works.

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit — ripped plywood or 1x4s also work.

steps.title: Build the inner frame
steps.body: Using 3" self-tapping wood screws, build the inner frame from the 2x4 pieces. Check for square by measuring diagonals corner to corner and adjusting until they match.

steps.title: Add the remaining joists
steps.body: Add the remaining joists to the inner frame, attaching with 3" self-tapping wood screws.

steps.title: Add the outer side trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the side trim to fit. Apply glue to the 2x4 frame and use 1-1/4" brad nails to attach the trim, leaving a 1-1/2" gap along the top edge.

steps.title: Add the outer end trim
steps.body: Repeat the previous step for the end trim.

steps.title: Attach the storage support
steps.body: Attach the storage support piece to the top of the inner frame with 3" self-tapping wood screws. This completes the platform.

steps.title: Build the storage box
steps.body: Cut the storage top and bottom pieces and attach at the corners with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, or with 2" screws or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add the dividers
steps.body: Set the dividers inside the top and bottom panels, inset 3/4", and attach with 1-1/4" nails and glue using a square as a guide.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Cut two 9-1/4"-long blocks and use them inside the box as a spacing guide for the shelves. Attach the two outer shelves with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails. For the middle shelf, toenail from the front at an angle into the vertical dividers.

steps.title: Add the planked back
steps.body: Mark the location of every divider and shelf on the inside lip of the box. Dry-fit the back boards to check the fit, then remove, apply glue to all edges, and reattach one board at a time, nailing through the outer sides into each back board's side and top edges.

steps.title: Join the platform and storage box
steps.body: It's easiest to finish the platform and storage piece separately before assembling, since the bed is large and difficult to move fully assembled. Use 2" self-tapping screws through the storage base into the frame, and into the storage support piece on the mattress side.

steps.title: Install feet and add center legs
steps.body: Install furniture feet or caster wheels per the manufacturer's instructions. If additional support is desired, add center legs cut from 2x4, spaced about a third of the way down, and attach with 3" self-tapping wood screws.

steps.title: Add the platform base and slats
steps.body: Cut the plywood platform base to fit, leaving a gap on the storage side, and screw down with 2" screws — drilling a few ventilation holes so the mattress can breathe. Cut the mattress slats and space them about 4" apart across the bed, screwing down with 2-1/2" screws.

images.alt: Platform bed with attached side storage tower and divider shelf
